title: Bromus kochii
intro: Bromus kochii, commonly known as Koch's brome or Koch's bromegrass, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Bromus kochii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Bromus kochii include decoctions and infusions, as well as extracts in capsule or powder form, which are used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Bromus kochii is a species of grass in the family Poaceae, commonly known as the poaceae family. It belongs to the genus Bromus, which contains around 160 species of grasses. The genus Bromus is part of the subfamily Pooideae. This classification places Bromus kochii within the larger group of grasses. The plant's classification also indicates its close relationships to other species within the same family and genus.
